figures.caption: Figure 21. Overview of the proposed DiT-IBGS.
Figure 1. Visualizations of clean-degraded pairs in Track 1 & 2. Each track features 7 distinct scenes from the RealX3D benchmark [29].

Table 1. Quantitative results of the 3DRR Challenge for Track 1 and Track 2, with teams presented in descending order of rank. Gray indicates teams that did not submit a valid factsheet and were therefore excluded from this report.

Figure 18. Overview of GenSmoke-GS. Smoke-degraded multiview images are sequentially restored via ConvIR-UDPNet, DCPbased dehazing, illumination correction, and MLLM-guided refinement under structural constraints, then reconstructed using 3DGS-MCMC with a FasterGS backend with ensembling.

Figure 25. Overall architecture of EE-GS. Degraded views are first restored by EE-Net, and then processed by Depth-AnythingV2 to estimate dense depth maps. Finally, the restored images together with the depth estimates are used in 3DGS for scene optimization.

Figure 26. The framework of 3DSmokeR. Smoked-degraded images are preprocessed in Stage 1 using a fine-tuned MBTaylorFormer [35] to restore clean input views. These enhanced images are then directly fed in Stage 2 into the water-splatting framework for robust 3D reconstruction and high-quality NVS.
Figure 4. Overview of the proposed TCIDNet-IBGS.

Figure 5. Overall architecture of IDEAL. The Appearance MLP predicts Gain and Bias maps to absorb view-dependent artifacts from Pseudo RGB during training, supervised by Lcolor and Ldepth. At inference, the Appearance MLP is discarded and only the clean Base 3DGS is retained for rendering.

Figure 22. Overview of the proposed DePhy-GS pipeline. The method progressively removes smoke degradation via dehazing and deep enhancement, followed by a hybrid 3D Gaussian Splatting representation with residual modeling. A lightweight physical model is used to describe the degradation process.

Figure 23. Overview of SmokeGS-R. Refined DCP inversion provides pseudo-clean supervision for a clean-only 3DGS, with four donor branches supplying appearance priors. At inference, a geometric-mean reference from five renders harmonizes the output in LAB space with light Gaussian smoothing.

Figure 7. Overview of GREP-GS. Pseudo-enhanced targets and relative depth priors are first extracted from low-light inputs, then 3DGS is optimized in a canonical display space using global and per-view adapters to handle view-specific enhancement bias.
